name: Jonathan Shelley
role: Lead Pastor, Stedfast Baptist Church, Watauga, Texas
incident: Jonathan Shelley, lead pastor of Stedfast Baptist Church, told an Arlington City Council meeting that God has ruled homosexuality is a crime worthy of capital punishment. Shelley's church website explicitly states the government should impose the death penalty for homosexuality. Shelley's own documented personal conduct, which involved soliciting prostitutes and gambling, subsequently forced the church to split.

name: Kenneth Copeland
role: Founder and Senior Pastor, Eagle Mountain International Church, Newark, Texas
incident: Kenneth Copeland, one of the wealthiest televangelists in America with a reported net worth exceeding $760 million and three private jets paid for by ministry funds, conducted a televised ceremony on April 5, 2020 in which he claimed to blow the wind of God through the television screen to destroy COVID-19. Copeland has publicly stated that flying commercial is flying in a tube with a bunch of demons, then asked followers to fund his private jet fleet through donations.

name: Jesse Duplantis
role: Founder, Jesse Duplantis Ministries, Destrehan, Louisiana
incident: Jesse Duplantis, a Louisiana televangelist, broadcast a video on May 28, 2018 asking followers to fund a $54 million Dassault Falcon 7X private jet for his ministry. Duplantis argued that Jesus would not ride a donkey today and that having a private jet allows him to spread the gospel faster. Duplantis already owned three private jets at the time of the fundraising request.
